cities.name: Copenhagen
cities.notes: Nyhavn canal, Tivoli Gardens, Little Mermaid and New Nordic dining

cities.name: Aarhus
cities.notes: ARoS Art Museum rainbow panorama and Den Gamle By open-air museum

cities.name: Odense
cities.notes: Hans Christian Andersen birthplace museum and Funen Village

cities.name: Aalborg
cities.notes: Viking burial site at Lindholm Hoje and waterfront Utzon Center

itinerary.sim: 3 (Three) Tourist SIM is the simplest tourist option in Denmark — about ₹2,200 for short stays with enough data for maps, DSB/DOT tickets, and hotspot backup.

Copenhagen.name: Copenhagen
Copenhagen.note: Three days gives you two compact city days plus a Roskilde day trip; buy DSB/DOT tickets in apps and reserve Tivoli or castle slots online in peak summer.

days.note: Keep this day walkable: Nyhavn, Strøget, Rundetårn and Torvehallerne are a compact loop. Add the canal tour early or late to avoid midday crowds at the harbour.

activities.name: Nyhavn harbour walk — colourful canal houses & photo stops
activities.tip: Arrive before 9am for calm reflections and empty quay photos; eating on Nyhavn itself is scenic but pricey.

activities.name: Copenhagen canal tour — harbour, opera house & Christianshavn from the water
activities.tip: Netto-Bådene is usually the value option; sit on the right side leaving Nyhavn for opera-house views.

activities.name: Strøget & side-lane design stores — pedestrian shopping spine
activities.tip: Detour into Illums Bolighus and Hay House for Danish design without committing to a museum.

activities.name: Rundetårn (Round Tower) — spiral ramp to old-city views
activities.tip: Go near sunset; the ramp is easier than stairs and works well between Strøget and Torvehallerne.

activities.name: Torvehallerne Market — smørrebrød, coffee and flexible food stalls
activities.tip: Great for mixed diets: one person can get seafood, another porridge or veg bowls, with no reservation needed.

days.note: Royal sights sit in a north-centre cluster; start with Rosenborg, walk via Amalienborg, then choose the National Museum before an evening Tivoli slot. Pre-book Tivoli if visiting Friday/Saturday nights.

activities.name: Rosenborg Castle — crown jewels and King's Garden
activities.tip: Book the first timed entry and see the Treasury before tour groups arrive.

activities.name: Amalienborg Palace square — royal guard change and Marble Church views
activities.tip: The guard change reaches the palace around noon; combine with the Marble Church dome if open.

activities.name: National Museum of Denmark — Vikings to Danish design
activities.tip: Prioritise the Viking/prehistoric rooms; it is too large to do properly after lunch if you linger everywhere.

activities.name: Christianshavn & Freetown Christiania walk
activities.tip: Use metro to Christianshavn; respect no-photo zones in Christiania and keep valuables zipped in busy lanes.

activities.name: Tivoli Gardens — historic amusement park after dark
activities.tip: Entry-only works if you mainly want gardens and lights; buy a ride pass only if you will do 3+ rides.

days.theme: Roskilde Day Trip: Vikings & UNESCO Cathedral
days.note: Roskilde is the easiest high-value day trip: 25–30 min by DSB train from København H, then walk or bus between the cathedral and harbour museum. Keep dinner back in Copenhagen instead of adding a third Roskilde sight.

activities.name: DSB train to Roskilde — quick suburban rail day trip
activities.tip: Check Rejseplanen for the fastest regional train and tap in/out correctly if using Rejsekort-style tickets.

activities.name: Roskilde Cathedral — UNESCO royal burial church
activities.tip: Start here from the station; the royal chapels are the reason to pay rather than just viewing the exterior.

activities.name: Viking Ship Museum — five original Viking ships and boatyard
activities.tip: Summer boat trips sell separately and fill quickly; book on arrival if you want to row/sail.

activities.name: Roskilde harbour walk — fjord views between museum and town
activities.tip: Use it as the scenic connector back toward town rather than adding a taxi.

meals: Café Knarr at Viking Ship Museum

days.theme: North Coast Day Trip: Hamlet's Kronborg & Louisiana
days.note: Ride the coastal regional train north along the Øresund: stop at Humlebæk for the Louisiana museum, then continue ~10 min to Helsingør for Kronborg. A DSB day ticket or Rejsekort covers the whole run (~45–55 min each way, trains every 20 min).

activities.name: Kronborg Castle, Helsingør — Renaissance fortress & Shakespeare's 'Elsinore'
activities.tip: UNESCO-listed; explore the dark casemates under the castle and catch the open-air Hamlet performances if visiting in summer.

activities.name: Louisiana Museum of Modern Art, Humlebæk — art & seafront sculpture park
activities.tip: One of Europe's finest modern-art museums; the sculpture garden over the Øresund and the café terrace are the highlight — it's right by Humlebæk station en route to Helsingør.

activities.name: Helsingør old town & M/S Maritime Museum of Denmark
activities.tip: Cobbled lanes run from the station to the castle; the award-winning maritime museum sits in a dry dock and its sunken plaza is free to wander.

activities.name: Ferry hop to Helsingborg, Sweden (20 min each way)
activities.tip: A fun optional bonus — the ForSea ferry crosses to Sweden in 20 min, so you can set foot in a second country and be back for dinner.

Aarhus.name: Aarhus
Aarhus.note: Use Aarhus for a compact two-day culture stop with most central sights walkable; Moesgaard and the beaches need a bus/taxi and should not be crammed into the ARoS/Old Town day.

days.theme: ARoS, Latin Quarter & Den Gamle By
days.note: ARoS, the Latin Quarter and Den Gamle By form a realistic city-centre day. Start at ARoS, eat nearby, then give Den Gamle By the afternoon because its open-air houses reward slower wandering.

activities.name: ARoS Aarhus Art Museum — Your rainbow panorama rooftop
activities.tip: Go straight to the rainbow walkway first; afternoon light is best for the coloured city views.

activities.name: Latin Quarter walk — cobbled lanes, boutiques and cafés
activities.tip: Use it as a coffee-and-shopping bridge between ARoS and dinner; most shops close earlier than Copenhagen.

activities.name: Den Gamle By — open-air old town museum
activities.tip: Seasonal pricing varies; arrive with enough time for the 1974 town district and not just the oldest houses.

activities.name: Aarhus Street Food — casual dinner hall in the bus garages
activities.tip: Good low-friction choice after museums, with Indian, Middle Eastern and vegan-friendly stalls.

days.note: Do not pair Moesgaard with Den Gamle By — the bus ride south and the museum itself need half a day. Return to the centre for Dokk1 and the harbour instead of zig-zagging across town.

activities.name: Moesgaard Museum — archaeology under a grass-roof landmark
activities.tip: Buy online to save a little and climb the sloping roof for a free view over woods and sea.

activities.name: Moesgaard forest and beach walk
activities.tip: Only add this in dry weather; trails are wooded and can be muddy after rain.

activities.name: Dokk1 & Aarhus waterfront — harbour library, bells and architecture
activities.tip: The public library is the best free harbour viewpoint and a reliable rainy-day reset.

activities.name: The Infinite Bridge / Marselisborg coast
activities.tip: Usually installed in the warm season; skip if the bridge is not in place or if winds are strong.

Odense.name: Odense
Odense.note: Odense works best as a one-night stop focused on Hans Christian Andersen heritage; keep the day central, using a short bus/taxi only for Funen Village if weather is good.

days.theme: Hans Christian Andersen Trail & Funen History
days.note: Start at H.C. Andersen House before the family crowds, then keep the cathedral and old town on foot. Choose Funen Village for open-air history; the Railway Museum is the rainy-day swap near the station.

activities.name: H.C. Andersen House — immersive museum in the fairy-tale quarter
activities.tip: Pre-book a morning slot; the audio-led experience is slower than a normal museum.

activities.name: St Knud's Cathedral and old-town lanes
activities.tip: Pair the cathedral with the cobbled streets around Overgade and the river before lunch.

activities.name: The Funen Village — open-air rural Denmark museum
activities.tip: Best May–September when costumed activities run; take the bus or a short taxi from the centre.

activities.name: Danish Railway Museum — vintage trains by Odense station
activities.tip: Use this instead of Funen Village in heavy rain or if travelling with train-loving kids.

hotels.name: Comwell H.C. Andersen Odense Dolce by Wyndham

connections.method: DSB InterCity/InterCityLyn train from København H to Aarhus H, usually just under 3 hours; book Orange fares early.

modes.note: Fastest door-to-door for most travellers; Orange fares sell out first.
FlixBus is cheapest; Kombardo Expressen uses the Molslinjen ferry and is more scenic.
Bridge toll plus fuel; useful only if continuing to rural Jutland.

modes.cost: ₹6,000–₹13,000 car+ferry (per car) or ₹1,600–₹3,700 pp by Kombardo
modes.note: Molslinjen via Sjællands Odde adds a sea crossing and can beat the bridge in traffic.
Only worthwhile if connecting through CPH/AAR; train is usually simpler.

connections.method: DSB intercity train from Aarhus H to Odense, about 1.5 hours; Orange fares can be very cheap if booked ahead.

modes.note: Most reliable and easiest city-centre to city-centre option.
FlixBus can undercut the train if booked early.
Simple motorway leg if you already have a rental car.

connections.skipped: Flights are not viable for this short city-pair; there is no useful direct air route.

extras: Prices were refreshed for July 2026 using about 1 DKK ≈ ₹14.7–₹14.8; round local prices up slightly for card fees and exchange spread.
Use DOT Tickets inside Copenhagen and Rejseplanen nationwide; DSB Orange train fares are departure-specific and cheapest when booked early.
Copenhagen hotel rates rise sharply around weekends, cruise peaks and summer concerts — book central budget hotels early or sleep near the metro.
Kombardo Expressen and Molslinjen are useful if you want the Copenhagen → Aarhus ferry experience; DSB remains the simplest luggage-friendly default.
Most Danish museums are child-friendly and many are free for under-18s, but adult tickets are expensive by Indian standards; plan no more than two paid museums per day.
Vegetarian travellers do well in Copenhagen/Aarhus food halls; Odense is easier with Storms Pakhus, supermarkets and cafés than with late-night restaurants.

Odense: Funen Village Odense open air museum

diet.vegetarian: Very manageable in Copenhagen and Aarhus: bakeries, porridge cafés, food halls and supermarkets clearly mark 'vegetar' options, and dairy/egg-based dishes are common. Odense is smaller, so plan lunches around Storms Pakhus, cafés or Netto/Føtex picnic supplies if dining late.
diet.vegan: Copenhagen has excellent vegan depth (Riz Raz, Morgenstedet-style cafés, vegan burgers, bakeries with oat milk); Aarhus is good around food halls and Latin Quarter cafés. In Odense, use HappyCow and supermarkets, ask for 'vegansk', and check that bread/pastries do not contain butter or egg.
phrases: Jeg er vegetar / veganer — I am vegetarian / vegan
Uden kød og fisk, tak — without meat and fish, please
Har I veganske retter? — do you have vegan dishes?
Uden mælk, æg og smør — without milk, eggs and butter
Er der gelatine eller fiskesauce i? — is there gelatin or fish sauce in it?
